(415) 557-1586 
May 4, 1981 
Ms. Barbara Milman 
Conflict of Interest Division Chief 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Re: 
Opinion No. 81-416 
Dear Ms. Milman: 
MAY 6 Ii 37 AM '01 
350 McALLISTER STREET 
SAN FRANCISCO 
94102 
(415) 557-2544 
Our opinion has been requested on the following 
question: 
Is it a conflict of interest for a County 
Supervisor who is also a licensed Real 
Estate Broker to act for either the buyer 
or seller in a real estate transaction 
which is contingent on County approval of 
such matters as compliance with Building 
Codes, obtaining conditional use permits, 
rezoning, etc.? 
This question presupposes that the Super-
visor will not in any way attempt to 
influence any County employee, will not 
participate in any application for such 
County approval and will, if the matter 
comes before the Board of Supervisors, 
disclose his interest in the transaction 
and disqualify himself from voting. 
Pursuant to our policy to request the views of 
interested parties and associations, you are invited to submit 
whatever views you may desire on the question prese.nted. 
Also, 
if you do not wish to submit any views, please so advise us. 
My administrative deadline for the prepa_ration of a 
response is June 5, 1981. 
Therefore! your prompt attention 
to this letter will be appreciated. 
CPR:lv 
Very truly yours, 
GEORGE DEUKMEJIAN 
Attorney General 
Deputy Attorney General

Pursuant to our policy to request the views of 
interested parties and associations, you are invited to submit

My administrative deadline for the prepa_ration of a

Therefore! your prompt attention 
to this letter will be appreciated. 
CPR:lv
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a County Supervisor who is also a licensed Real Estate Broker may act as an agent for either party in a real estate transaction that requires County approval, under the presumption that the Supervisor will not influence County employees or participate in the approval process and will disqualify himself if the matter comes before the Board of Supervisors.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document contains both an incoming request letter from Attorney General (May 4, 1981) and an FPPC response letter (May 14, 1981). Extracted the incoming request portion as the primary document. The FPPC response is fragmentary/partially legible but appears to indicate no conflict exists under stated conditions. Confidence reduced due to image quality and fragment visibility in response section.

embedding.summary: The California Attorney General's office (George Deukmejian) submitted a request to the FPPC seeking guidance on whether a County Supervisor's dual role as a licensed Real Estate Broker creates a conflict of interest when transacting in matters requiring County approval, with safeguards including non-interference and disqualification from voting.
